Fear and Greed sits at 

 Extreme Fear
and BTC is near
$62K
this morning, down from $70,000 earlier this week. BTC found a
partial floor overnight after the US Treasury Secretary told the
Senate the government is actively building a strategic Bitcoin
reserve.

The week's selling has been severe. But the policy signals
coming out of Washington are the most constructive in years. A
rare physical Bitcoin just moved after fifteen years. And Zcash
quietly fixed a critical bug while its price kept climbing.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Citi Says Bitcoin's Demand Problem Is Bigger Than One Sale

Citi says Bitcoin's decline is driven by weak investor
demand, not Strategy's small Bitcoin sale. ETF flows explain
roughly 45% of weekly Bitcoin return variation, the bank
estimates.

The current outflow streak is the longest since spot Bitcoin
ETFs launched in 2024. Without CLARITY Act progress, Citi warns
sentiment may stay muted.

What does Citi say could bring fresh demand back into Bitcoin?

Read More

74044.subject: PFI Monthly - May Edition
74044.body:
PFI Monthly 

РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Prague Finance Institute


РҗРІСӮРҫСҖ: Prague Finance Institute

    
    
    РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°Р№СӮРө СҶСҺ СҒСӮР°СӮСӮСҺ РҪР° LinkedIn, СүРҫРұ РҝСҖРёС”РҙРҪР°СӮРёСҒСҸ РҙРҫ РҫРұРіРҫРІРҫСҖРөРҪРҪСҸ
РҹСҖРҫСҮРёСӮР°СӮРё РҪР° LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/pfi-monthly-may-edition-prague-finance-institute-6lqvf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BD61o8EPuTCqqxsY8x9mV%2Fg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3j3tL4gM2iQIg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpzdu0ez~g7-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpzdu0ez-g7
  
Dear professionals, welcome to the new edition of our PFI newsletter. In the March issue, we have prepared for you: PFI Monthly research report PFI Roundtable PFI Talks PFI Monthly In this edition, we cover topics from financial markets, macroeconomics, corporate finance, private equity, banking, behavioral finance, and the Czech M&A market. The May edition also includes our interview of the month with TomГЎЕЎ Pfeiler, CFA, discussing the current AI-driven market environment, equity valuations, interest rates, government bonds, private credit, and broader risks across global financial markets. In this edition: Interview of the month вҖ“ TomГЎЕЎ Pfeiler, CFA: AI euphoria is the prevailing market narrative once again Trade-weighted real effective exchange rate (REER) as an essential analytical concept for every economist Simple, not easy: Every stock return has just two moving parts The return of the discount rate Equity investing 101 вҖ“ Investors have to distinguish between business performance and market pricing Efficient market hypothesis (EMH) and expectations investing Capital allocation вҖ“ The CEOвҖҷs most important job Seyfor enters CRM with 40% stake in Raynet at close to CZK 1bn valuation Private equity plays the long game Private equity is financing liquidity What covered bond pricing reveals about the Czech market Prospect theory вҖ“ The intellectual foundation of behavioral finance Book review of the month вҖ“ Mastering the Market Cycle: Getting the Odds on Your Side by Howard Marks рҹ‘ү Read the full edition to explore all insights in detail. PFI ROUNDTABLE | The ideal EXIT through the eyes of legal advisors and private equity investors What does a successful investment exit look like from the perspective of investors and legal advisors? And what is everything that goes into successfully completing a transaction? At the next PFI Roundtable meeting, we will open one of the key topics in the world of private equity and M&A вҖ“ the ideal EXIT. Insights from major transactions and investments will be shared by leading experts from practice: в—ҫпёҸ Jana Chwaszcz, Local Partner, White & Case LLP в—ҫпёҸ Petr Rieger, Director, The Carlyle Group в—ҫпёҸ Jan ДҢernГҪ, Managing Partner, Pale Fire Capital Moderator: Petr Skocdopole After the discussion, there will be informal networking. рҹ“ҚWednesday, June 10, 2026 I 18:00 Zenwork PalГЎc ARA Registration here! C

74042.subject: Team1 Applications Are Open: Build The Future Of Avalanche
74042.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/team1-applications-are-open-build

Team1 has spent the past few years doing the ground-level work of making Avalanche more accessible. 450+ members across 40 countries, 6 official chapters, 600+ community events, 75,000+ Luma subscribers growing 15% month over month, and collaborations with 130 universities. Six country chapters are live, with at least 6 more set to launch before the end of 2026.
Now, Team1 is looking for the next group to grow the Avalanche ecosystem.
Applications opened May 27th and close June 16th.
Why This Moment
AvalancheвҖҷs ecosystem is moving fast, and the gaps where community support makes a real difference are getting harder to ignore. New L1s are launching, developer tooling is maturing, and the user base is expanding into regions that are just starting to find their footing. Team1 is growing its core global team and rolling out 12+ new regional chapters this year.
The people who join now will shape what that expansion looks like.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
What Team1 Actually Does
Team1 is a global community built around growing the Avalanche ecosystem through education, events, content, and direct support for builders and newcomers. Members work in their local regions and across time zones to help people understand Avalanche, establish a presence, and join the ecosystem.
What that looks like varies. Some members run hackathons or university workshops. Others write guides, produce content, or help onboard developers and founders. Some show up through Discord, X, regional calls, or by connecting builders with the right people at the right time. Team1 supports all of it: event funding pathways, content bounty opportunities, creative support, and direct connections across the ecosystem.
ItвҖҷs real work that impacts the ecosystem.
The Kind of Person Team1 Is Looking For
Team1 is built for people who show up consistently in ways that fit their schedule and their strengths. You donвҖҷt need to be a full-time Web3 professional or someone who can host events every week. Whether you are a founder, event host, developer, graphic designer, videographer, or business development professional, we are looking for people who bring their unique skills to the table.
Integrity matters most. Members represent the community, and that carries weight. Beyond that, Team1 looks for people who take initiative without being asked, who are willing to try something new even when it doesnвҖҷt land perfectly, and who want to help others navigate the ecosystem rather than just building their own profile.
Collaboration is built into how Team1 operates. Members work across regions and countries, share ideas, and support each otherвҖҷs local efforts. If you find satisfaction in helping something or someone grow, youвҖҷll fit in here.
What You Get Out of It
Team1 gives you the infrastructure
